title: Multi-target delivery
principles: Multi-target capability requires explicit evidence such as explosion, radial reach, Punch Through, chaining, spread, or another supplied mechanic.
The primary job may be group_clear when the complete attack pattern reliably reaches several enemies rather than only describing a sustained firing rhythm.
Multi-target capability does not automatically imply area control; area control requires evidence that the weapon influences space, movement, or repeated zones.
Chaining should be evaluated from the supplied number of additional targets, chain range, and damage decay when available.
Punch Through can allow one attack path to affect multiple aligned targets, but it does not provide radial coverage.
Pellet spread may affect several nearby targets, but it can also concentrate on one target; do not classify reliable group clear without supporting evidence.
Explosion radius describes spatial reach, while falloff or secondary restrictions determine retained value.
Do not infer multi-target behavior from the mere presence of a special mechanic; the text or structured fields must explicitly support it.
exceptions: Do not treat every special mechanic as area or chaining evidence.
Do not assume chained targets receive full primary-target damage.
Do not call a weapon area_control solely because it can damage more than one enemy.
